description_long: This object is a streamlined, single-propeller airplane with a classic, vintage design. The fuselage is elongated and tapers smoothly toward the tail, with a wooden-textured nose section and a metallic or painted body. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, extending symmetrically from the fuselage with a slight dihedral angle, and the tail features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The propeller is mounted at the front, and the cockpit is positioned just behind the nose, likely enclosed but with minimal visible canopy detail. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels under the wings and a small tailwheel. The overall appearance suggests a light, general aviation or early military trainer aircraft, suitable for display or use in an urban park or museum setting.
description: A streamlined, single-propeller airplane with a wooden nose, metallic body, mid-mounted straight wings, and conventional tail. Features fixed landing gear and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated fuselage, wooden-textured nose, mid-mounted wings, and tailwheel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, symmetrical wing span, and narrow fuselage profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the tail and landing gear.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight, slightly dihedral wings and no visible winglets or sweep.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit positioned just behind the nose, likely enclosed and single-seat.
